title: How to Watch Oscars Red Carpet 2026
summary: Here's how to watch the red carpet at the 2026 Academy Awards.
story: The 98th Academy Awards are less than an hour away, and A-listers have officially hit the red carpet.

This year’s awards ceremony will once again be hosted by Conan O’Brien, while the nominees feature some of Hollywood's heaviest hitters, including Leonardo DiCaprio, Timothée Chalamet, Sean Penn, Michael B. Jordan, Emma Stone, Kate Hudson and Elle Fanning.

“Sinners” is up for 16 nominations, including best picture, making it the most nominated movie in Oscars history, while “One Battle After Another” is next with 13 nods. “Frankenstein," “Sentimental Value” and "Marty Supreme" notched nine and “Hamnet” scored eight.

Before the hardware is handed out and the acceptance speeches are made, though, you can get a peek at the stars in attendance when they walk the red carpet, which is traditionally lush with celebrities wearing memorable and iconic outfits.
Here’s what you need to know to gear up for Hollywood's biggest night.
When Are the 2026 Oscars?
The official 2026 Oscars ceremony will take place live from the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
How Can I Watch the 2026 Oscars Red Carpet?

ABC will broadcast a live, 90-minute red-carpet special, “On The Red Carpet at the Oscars: Countdown to Oscars,” beginning at 3:30 p.m. ET. “On the Red Carpet at the Oscars” then takes place at 5 p.m. ET before “The Oscars Red Carpet Show” takes over at 6:30 p.m. ET, leading into the ceremony.
You can watch those shows on ABC or stream them live on OnTheRedCarpet.com and via the On the Red Carpet Facebook and YouTube pages.
E! will also offer its own red carpet special, beginning at 4 p.m. ET.
How Can I Watch the 2026 Oscars?
O'Brien, who hosted the Academy Awards in 2025, will return to emcee the show this year. The event will air on ABC and can also be streamed on ABC.com and Hulu.
The show will also be available to stream on Hulu, YouTube TV, AT&T TV and Fubo. Several of these services offer free trials.

title: What Channel Are the Oscars On? How to Watch the 2026 Ceremony
summary: Here's everything you need to know about watching the 2026 Oscars.
story: Hollywood’s biggest night is nearly here. The 98th Academy Awards are set to honor the best in cinema over the last year.

“Sinners” has already made history by becoming the most nominated film in Oscars history, garnering 16 nominations, including one for the night’s biggest prize, best picture. “One Battle After Another” was next with 13 nods, while “Frankenstein,” "Sentimental Value" and "Marty Supreme" picked up nine.

Ten films make up the best picture category, with “Sinners,” competing against some critically acclaimed fare, including “Bugonia,” “F1,” “Frankenstein,” “Hamnet,” “Marty Supreme,” “One Battle After Another,” “The Secret Agent,” “Sentimental Value” and “Train Dreams.”

Michael B. Jordan was nominated for best actor for his role in “Sinners” in a stacked field that also features Leonardo DiCaprio (“One Battle After Another”), Ethan Hawke (“Blue Moon”) and Timothée Chalamet (“Marty Supreme”), who was nominated last year for “A Complete Unknown.” Wagner Moura rounds out the category for “The Secret Agent.”

The best actress race includes Jessie Buckley (“Hamnet”), as well as Rose Byrne (“If I Had Legs I’d Kick You”), Kate Hudson (“Song Sung Blue”), Renate Reinsve (“Sentimental Value”) and two-time Oscar winner Emma Stone (“Bugonia”).
This year’s ceremony also marks a first: a new category for casting.
Conan O’Brien, who hosted last year’s show, will return in the same role to oversee the ceremony.
What Day Are the 2026 Oscars?
The Oscars will take place Sunday, March 15.
When Do the 2026 Oscars Start?
The Oscars begin at 7 p.m. ET.
How to Watch the 2026 Oscars
The 2026 Oscars will air live on ABC and stream live on Hulu. You can also watch on ABC.com or the ABC app.
The ceremony will also be available to stream on Hulu,  YouTube TV, AT&T TV and Fubo. Several of these services offer free trials.
How to Watch the Oscars Red Carpet Pre-Show

A live, 90-minute red-carpet special, “On The Red Carpet at the Oscars: Countdown to Oscars,” will air on ABC starting at 3:30 p.m. ET. That will be followed by “On the Red Carpet at the Oscars” at 5 p.m. ET. Then, “The Oscars Red Carpet Show” comes on at 6:30 p.m. ET before into the ceremony begins.
You can watch the shows on ABC or stream it live on OnTheRedCarpet.com and via the On the Red Carpet Facebook and YouTube pages.
E! will also broadcast its own red carpet special, beginning at 4 p.m. ET.

title: 'Secret Lives of Mormon Wives' Star Jessi Ngatikaura Reveals Status of Her Marriage to Jordan
summary: Jessi Ngatikaura is sharing an update on the status of her relationship with husband Jordan Ngatikaura after a tumultuous Season 4 of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives.”The newest episodes of the Hulu reality television series, which arrived March 12, followed the ups and downs of the couple’s marriage as Jessi and Jordan Ngatikaura continued to butt heads.
story: Jessi Ngatikaura is sharing an update on the status of her relationship with husband Jordan Ngatikaura after a tumultuous Season 4 of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives.”

The newest episodes of the Hulu reality television series, which arrived March 12, followed the ups and downs of the couple’s marriage as Jessi and Jordan Ngatikaura continued to butt heads. It is a dynamic viewers have come to expect, with drama from Season 3 spilling over into Season 4.
“The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star addressed her ongoing marital struggles and revealed if the two are still together in an interview with “Entertainment Tonight.”

“I felt like this season it was important to share the continuation of what happened in Season 3 because it’s still hard,” Jessi Ngatikaura shared. “We still have ups and downs and hard days and, you know, easier days. But that’s just real life.”

Fans watched the two argue in Season 3 amid rumors of infidelity. Jessi Ngatikaura said she signed up to be on a reality show, so she might as well share all parts of her life, including her marriage and the fallout from that scandal.
“I think it would be hypocritical not to,” she added.
She also explained why her husband stays at home and she leaves whenever they have an issue.
“It’s because he doesn’t want to leave. Let’s be honest. It’s me who’s like, ‘I’m out of here,’” she said. “What I’ve learned is, like, in fight or flight, I’m flight. He’s fight.”
Jessi Ngatikaura said while she wants space and would rather process the situation on her own, her husband prefers to keep arguing.
“Sometimes I come back. Sometimes I don’t,” she said and laughed.
She revealed that she has considered kicking him out, but it doesn’t seem like an option.
“I don’t think he’d leave. Like, I really don’t think he would,” Jessi Ngatikaura told “Entertainment Tonight.”
Despite their struggles, she clarified that they are together and trying to repair their relationship.
“We’re together. We’ve been back together for almost a year,” she continued. “We’re still working on it. We’re still in therapy. It’s still easy and hard at the same time.”
She said she realized she cannot just walk away from their marriage because they share three children together. She also mentioned that finances are impacting her decision.
“So yeah, prenups get ‘em,” she said.
In Season 3, which was released in November 2025, the couple separated for 90 days after Jessi Ngatikaura had an “emotional affair” with “Vanderpump Villa” star Marciano Brunette.
She confirmed that she had kissed Brunette a few times, but she denied they slept together. She said she met the “Vanderpump Villa” star after the separation.

During an interview with TODAY.com at the time, Jessi Ngatikaura said about the couple publicly discussing their relationship struggles, “When we started talking about it on camera, we both agreed that if we’re going to do this, we’re going to do it 100%. We’re going to own our s---. We’re going to talk about the mistakes we’ve made and take the audience along on the journey.”

She added, “Hopefully that can help someone, similar to what Jen and Zac (Affleck) did in Season 1 and 2. They kind of put it all out there and I think it’s strengthened their marriage over time. And I’m hoping it’ll do the same for us.”

title: ‘Tiny Tienda’: Grocery Store Doubles as Concert Venue to Showcase Local Artists
summary: Mexican mercadito Fiesta Fresh Market in New Castle, Delaware doubles as a concert venue for local Latin artists, inspired by NPR's Tiny Desk concerts.
story: If you walk into Fiesta Fresh Market in New Castle, Delaware, you might be treated to more than fresh produce and ingredients typical of a mercadito, or a boutique Mexican grocery store. A band might play you your own personal concert, right there in the aisles.
Jose Aguilar, who co-owns the store with his sister and father, books and organizes these concerts. It all started because he has a record label, VPS Music, and worked with artist named Danny Lux.

Back in 2023, for Hispanic Heritage Month, NPR’s Tiny Desk celebrated with an “El Tiny” takeover, featuring a selection of Latin artists, including Danny Lux. Aguilar was in attendance, and that’s where the seed of an idea was planted.

“It was really cool how it was like a fairly simple setup. It’s all acoustic and it brought the office together,” Aguilar tells TODAY.com. “When we opened up the grocery store, we were looking for ways to market it, and me already kind of being in the music scene, it was fairly easy to get bands to come in and do us the favor of playing at the store.”
The store has been open since 2024, but since early 2025, Aguilar has posted performances from singer-songwriters and bands on Fiesta Fresh Market’s TikTok, YouTube and other social media channels.

“Before, when the performances first started, it was almost like people were annoyed that we were doing it, but now, as we’ve had some bigger acts stop by, people get excited and they’ll start asking when the next one is, which is really nice,” he says, though shopping does tend to slow a bit during the concerts.
Aguilar says his family already had all the equipment to put on and record live shows, so it was kind of a no-brainer.
“My family has been in both the music and the grocery store business since forever,” he says. “So it’s cool to combine them in this way.”
The market music mogul has seen more attention coming to both of his businesses as a result. Both his customer base and comments section, it seems, are passing the vibe check.
“Tiny Tienda,” one TikTok user nicknamed the series.
“Who ever came up wit this idea deserves the world fr,” wrote another. “so cool.”

“On social media, we’ve seen that people engage with it really well, and it’s translating from social media to actual streaming,” Aguilar says, adding that one Mexican American band that performed in January, Ilusión, has gotten more than just streams.
“Through this, they got contacted recently by Atlantic Records and got invited to their offices in New York, which is pretty cool,” he says.
Jesus Manuel Beltran Mendez, Ilusión’s lead singer, appreciates the attention their performance — surrounded by bananas and bags of Takis — has gotten.

“I think it has done amazing work for us, especially now,” he tells TODAY.com, adding that it’s not just record-deal attention; they’re getting gigs, too. Thanks to Aguilar’s series, Ilusión will be performing at South by Southwest in Austin, Texas this year.
“It’s just something so different that it really does make an impact, like, compared to other people promoting their music,” Beltran Mendez says.

Aguilar says upcoming band performances include New York’s Whirlpool, a Mexican American band; a local Delaware band called Community Chapstick; and another from Philadelphia they’ve yet to announce that will be a first for the concert series.
“That one’s going to be interesting because it’s traditionally been just regional Mexican music, and this one’s more all-around alternative music in English,” he says.

title: Valerie Bertinelli Opens Up About the ‘Worst Year' of Her Life and What She Refuses to Grieve About
summary: Valerie Bertinelli opens up about her latest book, "Getting Naked" and how she still found joy and gratitude during the 'worst year' of her life.
story: 2024 wasn't a good year for Valerie Bertinelli. In fact, according to the actor and former Food Network star, it was one of her worst.
"I had four surgeries, I was going through emotional turmoil with some people in my life," Bertinelli tells TODAY.com during a sit-down interview.

Yet, despite being difficult, she says it was exactly what she needed to hit the reset button. That period of reflection and renewal prompted her latest book, "Getting Naked: The Quiet Work of Becoming Perfectly Imperfect," part memoir, part self-help guide.
"I wanted it to be a little book people could tuck in their purse and bring out for inspiration," she says of the book's initial premise.
"Then I thought, well, if I'm going to tell people how to love themselves, I better start loving myself, too," explains Bertinelli.

"I thought I was there and I just realized, after my worst year in 2024, that I think I have more work to do. So, I really got into intensive work and really worked hard to get down deep to the trauma, so I could finally make peace with it."
Part of that trauma is the sexual abuse Bertinelli writes about in "Getting Naked," explaining that it occurred when she was 11, and that the experience was "the wound that started it all."

"It doesn't matter who, when, or how it happened," she writes in the memoir, saying that what does matter is the lasting impact it had on her, which helped shape her beliefs about herself and, ultimately, left her feeling 'unlovable.'

"No one knew the secret I kept, or how it wove through my marriages and divorces, my body dysmorphia, my decades of dieting, weight gain and loss, buried emotions, my impostor syndrome," writes Bertinelli in "Getting Naked."
When asked if revealing something so deeply personal was difficult, Bertinelli says it took her a while to come to terms with including the revelation in her book.

"I thought if people are going to trust me with what I've been through, I need to be brutally honest about it. So, I got to a point where, listen, if it hurts me by saying this or if it helps somebody else by saying this, that's the bigger issue," she says.

"I want to help somebody and, realizing now since it's been out, all the DMs I'm getting about people feeling so seen and need other people to talk about this so that they don't feel so alone. It's been really fulfilling."
The Woman in the Mirror: ‘I Don’t Care! I Look Good!'

Bertinelli first rose to fame as Barbara Cooper in the hit '70s show, "One Day at a Time," a sitcom about a single mother, Anne Romano (Bonnie Franklin), raising two teen daughters, Barbara and Julie (MacKenzie Phillips).
The sudden fame and constant scrutiny over her looks led to Bertinelli's complicated relationship with her weight.

“I remember my fifth grade teacher patted me on the belly and said, ‘You might want to keep an eye on that,’" she recalls in a 2020 interview with People. “That was the first time I became really aware of my body.”

Since then, Bertinelli has struggled with her self image, both privately and publicly, going on a series of diets, participating in various weight-loss programs and even served as a Jenny Craig spokesperson for six years, appearing in commercials and displaying her bikini-clad body on the cover of People in 2009.
But now, at 65, she's officially over it, choosing to focus on the things in life that bring her joy rather than spend any more time worrying about her appearance.
"I'm about 20 pounds heavier than when I was on the cover of People in a bikini. If you would ask me if I was going to get 20 pounds heavier back then, I would have been horrified," says Bertinelli.

"Now, I'm like, 'I don't care! I look good! Who the f--k cares? Yes, I've got flappy arms, but I'm also 65, and I'm alive, and I'm a good, caring person, and I like my character, and I like the way I treat my family and my girlfriends, and I like the way that they treat me. And I love being surrounded by my animals, who love me unconditionally, and my family that loves me unconditionally," she explains.

"I have so much love and joy in my life that if I'm going to focus on how I think, physically, I look, what does that bring me? It brings me nothing but grief and it focuses on the wrong part of what we are as human beings. Like, who are we? How do we treat people? Are we kind?"
With that mindset, Bertinelli says it's much easier to be happy.
"Now, when I look at myself in the mirror or I see photographs, it's like, 'Who cares?' I'm smiling. I'm full of joy because life is beautiful. Even when it sucks, you always find pockets of joy."
So, rather than worry about her appearance, Bertinelli says she, instead, practices finding gratitude.

"Everything is a wonder to me. Life is a wonder. A sunset, a sunrise are just a wonder to me," she says. "I'm one of those people that stops when I see the moon, no matter what phase it's in, because I think that it's beautiful and I think the world is beautiful."
'Even in the Worst Year of My Life, I Had so Much Joy'

Even in the hardest of times, including 2024, Bertinelli says there were still plenty of things to be grateful for, including her 34-year-old son, Wolfgang Van Halen, her only child with ex-husband, guitarist, Eddie Van Halen, who died of a stroke in 2020 after a decades-long battle with cancer.

"If I wanted to focus on it, I could just say sh---y things are happening and I'm just going to feel like s--t. But I had these beautiful pockets of joy. I got to start the year sitting in the studio watching Wolfie create his art. I got to go on the road and watch him," she says.

Bertinelli also landed a recurring job on The Drew Barrymore Show, fulfilling a lifetime dream of living and working in New York City, which she commutes to from her Studio City, California, home, every other week.
"So even in the worst year of my life, I had so much joy. So, you can focus on that, and that is the key, I think, to life: focusing on the gratitude and the things you're grateful for."

In addition to her latest book, Bertinelli also recently launched Valerie's Place, a new subscription-based website that includes cooking shows, a podcast and other interactive experiences for fans. The "Hot in Cleveland" actor is also returning to the small screen in "Love, Again," a new Lifetime movie slated to be released later this spring.
With so much on her plate, Bertinelli says she still has unfinished business to tackle.

"I want everyone to feel strong and love themselves," says Bertinelli. "I just want to see the world a better place. I want to see the world full of people loving themselves more because that's how you spread that. There's a reason there's trolls out there because they're miserable in their lives and I don't want those people to be miserable."
A frequent target of "rude and awful" comments on social media, Bertinelli says that instead of feeling upset by them, she sympathizes with the people who target her.

"The first place I go is how sad that they need somebody to feel safe to lash out on," she explains, saying that if there were less vitriol in the world, it would be a much more beautiful place for everyone.
"I know I sound 'woo-woo' and rainbows and unicorns, but we can have joy in terrible times."

title: Who Is Ethan Hawke's Wife? All About His Love and Creative Partner, Ryan Shawhughes Hawke
summary: Who is Ethan Hawke's wife? Was she his nanny? He sets the record straight on how they met and their relationship origin story.
story: Ethan Hawke has enjoyed his fair share of Oscar nominations, most notably for roles in films like “Training Day” and “Boyhood." This Sunday, the actor is in the running for best actor for his work in “Blue Moon.”
Though the acting nomination is his own, the production of the film itself was done in collaboration with his wife and creative partner, Ryan Shawhughes Hawke.
In a 2009 interview with The Guardian, Ethan Hawke described his wife as “an extremely sensible, no-bulls--- woman,” and said “frankly I thought that might be good for a half-madman like me.”
He was previously married to the actor Uma Thurman, with whom he shares two children, Maya and Levon. Ethan Hawke and Thurman divorced in 2004.
She's a TV and Film Producer
Ryan Hawke is the vice president of Under the Influence Productions, a production company founded by her husband.
There, she is responsible for writing, developing and producing works for television and film, among other mediums, according to FX Networks.

Most recently the pair worked on FX’s “The Lowdown,” and Ryan Hawke was also involved in the production of “Blue Moon.” They worked together on the miniseries “The Good Lord Bird,” which Ethan Hawke starred in.
The couple first worked together on “Seymour: An Introduction,” a documentary about pianist and composer Seymour Bernstein, according to a January interview with the Los Angeles Times.

“We’d been married for a little while and we really like working together,” Ethan Hawke said during a panel discussion at the Sundance Film Festival hosted by the Los Angeles Times in January. “And we think the same way, and Ryan has unbelievable literary taste.”
And a Philanthropist, Too
Outside of production, Ryan Hawke is on the board of trustees for Romanian educational nonprofit The Alex Fund.
She is also the chair of the board of directors for EngenderHealth, which advocates globally for sexual and reproductive health and gender equality.
Was She His Nanny? Allow Ethan Hawke to Explain
In a 2009 interview with The Guardian, Ethan Hawke laid out how he met his future wife.
Ethan Hawke said they were connected through his literary agent, and she later watched his children during a film shoot.
“After a short period of working for me, Ryan went back to Columbia to get her degree," he said.
Ethan Hawke said they were not romantically involved at the time when they first met.
"In the years that followed, my marriage disintegrated due to many pressures, none of which were remotely connected to Ryan," he said.
About a year later, they crossed paths in a park. Ryan Hawke eventually asked him on a date, he said in the same interview.

“So when she asked me out, I answered, ‘Yes,’” Hawke said. “I know people imagine some kind of 'Sound Of Music' type love affair, but the truth is by the time Ryan and I were falling in love, it had been a long while since I had employed her.”
The couple married in 2008, according to People.
Ethan and Ryan Hawke Had 2 Kids Together
In an interview with The Times in 2025, Hawke described his attitude following his divorce: "I was hellbent on not having any more kids and I wanted to be single for the rest of my life."
Then, he met Ryan Hawke and things changed.
“But I made a best friend and I liked kissing her,” Hawke continued.
Today, the couple share two daughters. Their first daughter, Clementine, was born in 2008. Their second, Indiana, was born in 2011.
Ethan Hawke spoke about how his two older kids adjusted to their new siblings in a 2015 interview with The Times.

“I already had my kids from Uma and it was hard for them. Ryan was having our baby, and she was reading a book about step-parenting, and how difficult it can be, and that book was talking about rules,” Ethan Hawke said at the time. “People think that kids don’t like rules, but in fact they do, even if they’re just something to rebel against.”
Ryan Hawke, in the same interview, said of parenting, "I do think that the goal is to help the child feel comfortable and ready to succeed.”

title: Cher's Son Chaz Bono Marries Longtime Love Shara Blue Mathes
summary: Chaz Bono is officially married! He married longtime partner Shara Blue Mathes on March 8 with Cher in attendance.
story: Chaz Bono has a new title: husband.
The 57-year-old son of Cher and the late Sonny Bono tied the knot with his longtime partner, Shara Blue Mathes, on March 8.
Per the couple's wedding website, the celebration took place at The Hollywood Roosevelt in Los Angeles with a dress code of "Hollywood Glam Formal."
In an interview with People, Bono offered insight into the backstory of his romance with Mathes.

"I first met Shara more than 40 years ago when we were just teenagers," he said. "I think it’s safe to say that not many grooms ultimately end up marrying the very first girl they ever kissed so many years later."
After reconnecting years later, the duo officially became a couple in January 2017.
While reflecting on his love for his wife, Bono explained what makes their relationship work.

"She makes me feel complete knowing she will always be there beside me as partner, my best friend, and the love of my life. No matter what life brings us, we will always be safer and stronger together … and we are home," he said.

Per People, Cher attended the wedding and sat in the front row to watch her son exchange vows with his bride. The groom wore a tuxedo and black bow tie for the special occasion, while Mathes donned a white gown with lace sleeves.
Over the years, Mathes has shared several photos of herself with Bono and Cher on her Instagram page. In December 2024, she posed with the singer while celebrating the release of her memoir.
Mathes and Bono have shared several glimpses into their life at home on Instagram over the years. Mathes has also shared that she's a mother to a 25-year-old son named Cooper.
Bono is the eldest child of Cher, who also has a 49-year-old son named Elijah Blue Allman. She welcomed her second child with her ex-husband Gregg Allman.
Bono, a transgender man, was referred to by a different first name in "Cher: The Memoir, Part One," which the singer explained in an author's note.

“In this memoir, I refer to my son Chaz as Chas, the name he went by during the years covered in this book,” she wrote. “Chaz has granted his blessing for this usage. In the next volume, at the appropriate point, I will refer to my son as Chaz.”

title: This Major 'Virgin River' Character Isn't Coming Back Next Season, Showrunner Says
summary: Why Lauren Hammersley and Marco Grazzini, who play Charmaine and Mike Valenzula, are leaving Virgin River for Season 8.
story: It’s a bittersweet day for “Virgin River” fans.
The hit Netflix series returned for Season 7 on March 12 with 10 new episodes, delighting viewers. But the series’ showrunner also announced the departure of two characters.
In June 2025, Netflix announced that “Virgin River” was already renewed for Season 8, long before Season 7 even premiered.
In a new interview with Deadline, showrunner Patrick Sean Smith revealed that Marco Grazzini, who plays Virgin River police officer Mike Valenzuela, is leaving the series after five seasons.
“He’s not coming back for Season 8,” Smith said.

In recent seasons, Grazzini‘s character has ben romantically involved with attorney Brie (Zibby Allen), the sister of Jack (Martin Henderson). In Season 6, Mike proposed to Brie, even though he knew that she had cheated on him with her ex-boyfriend, Brady (Ben Hollingsworth).
Grazzini commented on his departure from the show in a statement shared with Entertainment Weekly.
"Character departures are very common in this business — especially on a long-running and beloved series like 'Virgin River.' It’s been a wonderful opportunity to play Mike for six seasons," he said.
Grazzini said he has "nothing but love for the cast, crew, and especially the phenomenal fans who make the show so special."
"I’m excited for this new chapter in my career," he said.
During his interview with Deadline, Smith also announced that another popular character, Charmaine (Lauren Hammersley), who went missing in the Season 6 finale, won’t be returning in Season 8.
Although Mike and Charmaine are saying farewell to “Virgin River” for now, that doesn’t mean they won’t return some day.
“But they’ll definitely be back and continue to be a big presence on the show,” Smith said about the characters.
Looking to the future, Smith said there’s always a possibility of other changes to the cast as the show evolves.
“For me, I’m just trying to look to the longevity of the show and making sure that the characters that we’re keeping have enough story engine in them to keep going,” he said.
“So I can’t say there are any specific cast shakeups planned, but I think we will have to start to — as characters run their course — make some decisions and bring in some new blood,” he said.

title: Mikayla Matthews and Husband Jace Terry Reveal Separation in Season 4 of ‘Secret Lives of Mormon Wives’
summary: Mikayla Matthews and husband Jace Terry of "The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives" have shared that they are separated after struggling with intimacy issues.
story: Mikayla Matthews and husband Jace Terry of "The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives" have shared that they are separated after  struggling with intimacy issues.

The couple revealed they are not together in Episode 9 of Season 4, which was released on Hulu on March 12. Matthews said in the episode that the couple continued to have troubles with their sex life after she shared in Season 3 that she had been sexually abused as a child. She was also dealing with an undiagnosed chronic illness while postpartum after having the couple's third child in July 2025.
"We both agreed to do a separation," Matthews said in a confessional in the episode.
The couple are then shown talking it out in their bedroom.
"At home you feel like you can’t focus on your skin and your health because I’m also here," Terry said.

"I think it’s hard any time you bring up the stuff, the sex, the intimacy, I feel like it’s hard on me because I feel like my nervous system is going into fight or flight, I’m already maxxed out," she replied. "Like my bucket is full."
While Terry suggested she "commit to trauma therapy," Matthews said past therapy work hasn't lessened her struggles.

"I don’t think it’s like with the intention of being separated forever. I think we’re obviously still going to see each other every single day. I’m not trying to go even a day away from the kids anyways," she said.
The couple have three children and have been married since 2018. Matthews was 17 years old when they had their first child.
Matthews expressed on the show that she does not know when her separation with Terry will end.

"I think it’s definitely hard to put a timeline on the situation because it could take weeks, it could take months to be healed," she said in a confessional. "There’s part of me that is definitely scared to lose him because I feel like our marriage is so good outside of the sexual intimacy part of it, which is ironic because that’s his No. 1 need and that’s my No. 1 wound."
Terry told her in the episode that "it would be better" for the kids if they worked things out.
Fellow castmember Mayci Neeley then picked up Matthews as she left her home. The couple shared a hug and a kiss goodbye.
"I don’t want to have to tear our family apart, but I also know that I have to be selfish for a period of time so that I can heal," Matthews said.
The reality star also spoke to People about the separation.

“Last season, we dove into the beginning stages of it, and that was me revisiting all this trauma for the first time as well,” she said about her past sexual abuse. “It’s still new to me, and it’s new to him, and we’re just navigating it the best way that we can. Unfortunately, I think it’s just something that’s going take a lot more time. I feel like that’s all I can say on it now.”
Earlier in the episode, she spoke with castmate Whitney Leavitt about her relationship with her husband.

"I feel like I'm at a point where like when I get really angry I'm like, 'OK, fine, then go have sex with someone. Like, leave me alone almost,'" she says. "I feel like I'm working on so many different things outside of just sex, and I know that obviously he can't just be like, 'OK, well we can just go without that.'

"We both get to a point where we feel really helpless about it, because he's like, 'I don't feel like you have the motivation to do it,' and I'm like, 'I don't think I have the motivation to do it right now because I know that it's really hard to work through.'"
Terry also spoke with Leavitt's husband, Conner, who has spoken out about his own experience with sexual abuse, for advice. During their conversation, Terry eventually broke down in tears.
"More than the actual sex I just want to feel like I can connect with my wife again, and I want to feel loved by my wife," he said.
While speaking to Whitney Leavitt, Matthews brought up the possibility that maybe she should "just go away from the house for like four weeks."
She then shared in a confessional that the couple stayed up late "talking out the pros and cons of separating" before ultimately deciding to do it.
"I'm definitely reaching a breaking point," she said.

title: How Accurate Is ‘The Dinosaurs’ on Netflix? A Paleontologist Tells Us
summary: A paleontologist tells TODAY whether Netflix's "The Dinosaurs" is accurate in its portrayal of these prehistoric creatures.
story: From “The Flintstones” to “Jurassic Park” to “The Land Before Time,” dinosaurs have captured people’s imaginations on screen for generations, but maybe not quite like they have in Netflix’s new four-part series “The Dinosaurs.”

Narrated by Oscar-winning actor Morgan Freeman and executive produced by Steven Spielberg — who has directed and produced multiple films and shows in the “Jurassic Park” franchise — “The Dinosaurs” is a feast for the eyes with its effects as it depicts these prehistoric reptiles. Freeman himself is blown away by the visuals.
“Absolutely. There’s no way not to be,” he told TODAY last month.
“You don’t believe it,” he told Jimmy Fallon about the imagery when he appeared on “The Tonight Show” last month.
What is 'The Dinosaurs'?
"The Dinosaurs" is a four-part series about the rise and fall of the dinosaurs.

“From the first dinosaurs to the last, this epic documentary series examines their 165 million years on Earth and the forces that shaped their evolution,” Netflix says in its description of the series.
Freeman himself sets the tone early in the show.
“Majestic creatures. Giants and monsters that can often seem more imagined than real,” he says in the beginning of “The Dinosaurs” about their presence roaming the planet 66 million years ago.
A Paleontologist Reviews 'The Dinosaurs' — and So Does the Public
The series has won raves among a group of people who know a thing or two about dinosaurs: Those in the scientific community.

“This is a technically and scientifically impressive series that clearly is one of the best dinosaur series that’s ever been made,” Dr. Steven Brusatte, a paleontologist and professor at Scotland’s University of Edinburgh, tells TODAY.com in an email.
“The Dinosaurs” is also a Tyrannosaurus rex-sized hit with viewers. The show has garnered a 100% rating on Rotten Tomatoes, while those on social media have also chimed in to praise it.
“Highly recommend, especially with kids,” one X user wrote.
“Highly recommended, the attention to detail is incredible,” someone else wrote.
How Accurate Is 'The Dinosaurs,' According to Experts?
So people like it, but is “The Dinosaurs” on point in telling their story?
Brusatte says the series takes some liberties, but it makes sense, given what we actually do know about the animals.

“The CGI dinosaurs are, generally speaking, lifelike and realistic, and while I can quibble with little things as a scientist, we have to accept that there is so much artistic license that must go into making programs like these, because there is so much we don’t know about exactly how dinosaurs behaved and moved and sounded and interacted, as the fossil record is limited,” he writes.
What’s With the Lack of Grass in 'The Dinosaurs'?
Some pterodactyl-eyed viewers have noticed there is no grass in the series, leading people to wonder if that was an error.
Brusatte says the series got that detail right.
“The first simple grasses appeared right at the end of the age of dinosaurs,” he writes. “But no grasslands until long after the dinosaurs were gone.”
Does 'The Dinosaurs' Honestly Tell the Story of What Happened to the Dinosaurs?
Dinosaurs have caused mayhem in film (see: "Jurassic Park"), but this show paints them in a different light.
Brusatte appreciates the narrative of “The Dinosaurs” and how it veers away from portraying them as vicious warriors.

“It’s a story of dinosaur evolution over time, and as a scientist who has studied dinosaur evolution and written about dinosaur evolution for the public, I always love seeing media that embrace that historical narrative of dinosaurs changing and adapting, and not just dinosaurs as monsters biting and scratching and fighting,” he writes.

title: Goo Goo Dolls Frontman John Rzeznik Reacts to '90s Trend Featuring 'Iris'
summary: What is the "90s" trend? Why is everyone setting montages to Iris by the Goo Goo Dolls. Frontman   John Rzeznik reacts to the trend
story: About two months after the internet was obsessed with the year 2016, now it’s the ‘90s that are in vogue. People on the internet are asking, and answering, a single question: What were you like in the ‘90s?

NBC’s Joe Fryer unpacked the trend that’s leaving people longing for life 30 years ago in a TODAY segment that aired on March 12. Some of the biggest ‘90s stars, from John Stamos to Drew Barrymore, have already participated. And the Goo Goo Dolls, whose song is included in the trend, are weighing in, too.
Here’s what to know about the “what were you like in the ‘90s?” trend.
Inside the ‘What Were You Like in the ‘90s?’ Trend
The trend is simple. People film themselves in the present day with questions like this atop the screen: “Mom, what were you like in the ‘90s?” or “Dad, what were you like in the ‘90s?”
Then comes a montage of photos and videos of the same person in the ‘90s. The Goo Goo Dolls classic “Iris,” playing over the photos and videos, heightens the nostalgia.

Celebrities, including ones who rose to fame in the ‘90s, are happily hopping on the trend. John Stamos shared memories from his “Full House” Uncle Jesse era, showing off his “bad boy” look and reminding social media why he was every girl’s first crush at the time. Kevin Bacon posted photos on Instagram flashing back to his long hair and leather jackets.
The celebrity participation list does not stop there, with more posts from Jewel, Reese Witherspoon, Brooke Shields, Joey McIntyre and music groups like the Spice Girls and the Backstreet Boys.

The trend is prompting reflection, too. Drew Barrymore wrote on Instagram that she “went down memory lane looking at these photographs.” Kristin Davis, who played Charlotte York on the HBO show “Sex and the City,” one of the defining cultural moments of the ’90s, said the era was a “very interesting time.”
Who’s Driving the Nostalgia?
The generations who’ve grown up online are the ones yearning for a time that was not-so-digital.
Gen Z and Gen Alpha are driving this ‘90s nostalgia trend, according to Clay Routledge, a leading expert on the science of nostalgia.

“They think there’s something really cool about the pre-digital life — about the analog, more-tactile in-person life — and they’re trying to get in touch with that and find inspiration from it, to find balance in their own digital lives,” Routledge told TODAY.
Younger generations have also been inspired by the FX and Hulu series “Love Story” about JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette’s ‘90s romance.
Gen Z and Gen Alpha are obsessed with Bessette’s minimalist style and JFK Jr. has been inspiring young men to don caps.
The Goo Goo Dolls React to the Viral Trend

The flashback montages are all accompanied by the same song: “Iris” By the Goo Goo Dolls. The song, which came out in 1998, also had a moment in 2025, and was deemed "the song of the summer" by the Wall Street Journal thanks to its resurgence on charts. Footage of Goo Goo Dolls frontman and guitarist John Rzeznik singing in a rainy concert in Buffalo has also gone viral.
The band is calling the most recent trend a “blast.”

“We’re so thrilled at the continued support of ‘Iris’ nearly 30 years after its release,” Rzeznik told TODAY. “The ‘90s trend is a blast for us and it’s so fun to see all the old school photos from some of our favorite people. “
“It’s also really cool to connect with a younger audience who are discovering not only “Iris” but a lot of our songs,” Rzeznik added. “It’s really been a great time and I’m super grateful.”

title: What Happened to Dermot Mulroney's Character on 'Chicago Fire'?
summary: The latest "Chicago Fire" storyline has one of its characters taking a break from Firehouse 51
story: The latest "Chicago Fire" storyline has one of its characters taking a break from Firehouse 51.
After a series of complicated events, Dermot Mulroney's character, Chief Dom Pascal, found himself in a sticky situation when he lost his job.
The plot move comes nearly two months after viewers learned Mulroney would be taking a hiatus from the show.
Is Mulroney leaving "Chicago Fire" for good? And what happened to his character? Here's everything you need to know.
What Happened to Chief Pascal?

Chief Pascal disobeyed a federal agent's orders while he was securing evidence at a house fire during a recent One Chicago crossover event, per NBC Insider. Pascal's actions ended with him getting arrested, but he was able to get the charges dropped.
But during Episode 14, which aired March 11, Pascal was terminated for unbecoming conduct and was told he was not fit to be a battalion chief.
Why Is Dermot Mulroney Taking a Hiatus from the Show?
In January, Deadline announced that Mulroney’s last episode in Season 14 would be Episode 14. Per the publication, the break will “take him into the end of the season” when “he may return."

Earlier this month, "Chicago Fire" showrunner Andrea Newman told NBC Insider the character would have to "figure out if he’s going to fight and claw to get back to 51, or if he’s now going to be on a new path altogether.”
Per Deadline, Mulroney's time away from the show is part of his character's storyline. The publication also noted that more information will be revealed as the season continues to air.
Who's In Charge of Firehouse 51 Now?
Pascal left his office to Lt. Kelly Severide (Taylor Kinney), serving as a symbolic way of saying he's the new battalion chief in his absence.
However, Deadline previously revealed that a new character, Battalion Chief Hopkins, played by Rob Morgan, will be joining the show while Mulroney is on hiatus.

Hopkins, who will first be seen in Episode 16, will appear in four episodes this season. Per Deadline, he has a "long, haunted past in overseeing several Chicago firehouses and is quick to assert authority over his colleagues."

title: The Pussycat Dolls Are Back! What to Know About 53-Date 'PCD Forever Tour'
summary: Don't cha love a Pussycat Dolls reunion? How to get tickets to their upcoming "PCD Forever Tour."
story: Don't cha love a Pussycat Dolls reunion?
After two days of cryptic Instagram posts featuring the words "PCD Forever," the Pussycat Dolls announced on March 12 that they are back for their first tour in 17 years.

The version of the group that will embark on the 53-date "PCD Forever Tour" across Europe and North America will be made up of three members, compared to the six-woman lineup that originally comprised the Pussycat Dolls before they disbanded in 2010. Lil' Kim and Mya will also appear as special guests.

Ashley Roberts, Nicole Scherzinger and Kimberly Wyatt announced on British radio show "Heart Breakfast" March 12 that the trio version of the group is ready to take the stage. Original members Jessica Sutta, Carmit Bachar and Melody Thornton are not part of the upcoming tour.
"I have a big announcement," Roberts said. "Now, I'm not here alone. I have brought some of my friends that you might have heard before."
Scherzinger and Wyatt then joined her on the show.
"Well, we're baaaaaack!" Roberts said.
"And we're going on tour," Scherzinger said.
Scherzinger spent 2023 through 2025 starring as Norma Desmond in an acclaimed London and Broadway production of "Sunset Boulevard," which earned her a Tony award.

"After I dedicated my every waking breath to theater over the past three years, I was like, 'This is amazing, this is so triumphant. And now I miss my girls, and I miss touring, so yeah we should do a world tour. That sounds about right,'" she said.
Wyatt is a reality star and actor who has become a mom of three children since the Pussycat Dolls initially disbanded.
"Those three kids, who are on the school run right now, and I did promise them: Willow, Maple, Senna, mommy loves you!" she said on the show. "And she's a Pussycat Doll again!"

The new tour comes seven years after the Pussycat Dolls' last performance in 2019, when they reunited during the season finale of “The X Factor: Celebrity." That version of the group also included Sutta and Bachar. They released the single "React!" as part of the reunion show.
At the time, they announced a nine-date reunion tour in 2020, which was ultimately scrapped due to the pandemic.
Famous for songs like "Don't Cha," "Buttons" and "Stickwitu," the latest iteration of the Pussycat Dolls released the new single "Club Song" to coincide with the tour announcement.

“After 20 years, you’re like, ‘Where is everybody?’ But for us, it aligned," Scherzinger told the BBC. "People are in different places in their lives, some of the other people have different commitments and priorities, but this worked out for us.”
Roberts told the BBC that it was Scherzinger who sparked the reunion by reaching out to her and Wyatt.

“We had some conversations, and we were just like, ‘We’re on the same page, we’re excited about embracing where we’re at now as women and celebrating that,'” she said. "I’m just really excited to get back on stage and swing these hips around in some latex, hun!”
The Pussycat Dolls' two albums, "PCD" and "Doll Domination," are also being reissued with an extended track mix and new remixes, according to the group's website.
How to Get Tickets to the 'PCD Forever Tour'
Tickets for the tour can be pre-ordered on the tour website, with presale starting March 18. Fans can sign up for the presale on Ticketmaster now until Monday, March 16, at 11 p.m. ET.
The general sale begins on March 20 at 10 a.m. local time, according to the group’s announcement.

title: 'Love Story' Hair Colorist Explains How They Turned Sarah Pidgeon's Brunette into Carolyn Bessette's Blond
summary: Here's how Love Story recreated Carolyn Bessette's blond hair even though star Sarah Pidgeon is a brunette.
story: If blondes have more fun, Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy made it look effortless. That, of course, was the magic trick.

In Ryan Murphy’s “Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette,” Sarah Pidgeon didn't just step into Bessette's perfectly tailored Calvin Klein staples. She traced the arc of Bessette's life through her hair.

When we first meet Carolyn in the show, her blonde is warmer, more dimensional, as if it were grazed by the sun. As the series progresses and she marries John John, we see her hair transform into a brighter platinum. The shift is subtle, but it mirrors everything else in Bessette's sphere at the time: the growing public scrutiny, the Kennedy refinement and the overall myth-making of an American icon.
Off-screen, the transformation required far more precision than the word “effortless” suggests.
The Evolving Blond of Carolyn Bessette

Pidgeon is a natural deep brunette, not exactly a casual leap away from the bright blonde that defined Bessette’s image. In fact, Pidgeon revealed on "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" that her team photoshopped blond hair onto a photo of her during the casting process to make the transformation seem plausible.
As colorist Kari Hill, who worked on the show, puts it to TODAY.com, taking someone “from dark to light is a loaded question.”
There are “so many different aspects” to lifting hair that dark, she explains, especially when you’re not just going blond but moving through multiple versions of blond across a timeline.
“It was very tedious. It was very thought out. It was a labor of love,” Hill says. Most importantly, they moved slowly “to make sure that the integrity of the hair stayed intact.”

Working alongside hairstylist Barry Lee Moe, Hill approached the process less like a dramatic makeover and more like character development. The team studied a detailed visual timeline of Bessette's life, mapping how her color evolved from the early ‘90s through the end of the decade.
In the early years, Bessette's blond carried more contrast, with warmth on top and deeper dimension underneath.
“There was a lot more dark undertones,” Moe explains. Later, it shifted into something brighter and more uniform, but always golden.

That warmth, both Hill and Moe emphasize, was essential. “We really leaned into warm tones and made sure we didn’t get too cool,” Moe says. On Pidgeon especially, going icy simply wouldn’t have worked.
More than anything, the blond had to be believable.

“If it doesn’t look real, if (the hair) feels like it’s sitting on top of them, you’ve already kind of missed the mark,” Moe says. Carolyn’s entire aesthetic, he adds, was what he calls “lived-in luxury.”
She wasn’t chasing glamour. “She just had an inner confidence that was untouchable,” and the hair had to reflect that.
The Technique Behind the Transformation
To achieve that balance of brightness and softness, Hill relied on a method she coined herself: foiled cashmere.

Inspired by Carolyn’s signature blond, the method updates classic ‘90s full-head foiling with ultra-precise placement and customized toning for seamless dimension and a soft, expensive looking finish.
This style marks a departure from the grown-out, “lived-in” blonds that have dominated the past decade. “It’s back to the basics,” Moe says. “Back to foils. Back to blonde color to the root.”

In other words, this isn’t the grown-out, beachy blond that has dominated the past decade. The brighter, later-’90s version especially requires commitment— regular maintenance and a willingness to stay on top of salon appointments.
The difference between then and now, Hill notes, is what makes it possible to do this without sacrificing the hair itself.

In the ‘90s, lighteners were “a lot more aggressive,” she says. Today’s bonding technology allowed her to lift Pidgeon gradually while protecting the strength and elasticity of her hair — crucial on a production schedule where, as Moe explains, “you can’t go back and forth” between colors.

The final, brightest version of Carolyn’s blond, the one that opens the series, couldn’t even be filmed until much later in the production timeline, after the final transformation had fully taken place.

Even lighting played a role. “The worst thing in the world is when you get them on set and suddenly the blond looks green,” Moe says. Fortunately, the smoky, muted palette of ‘Love Story’ “really lent itself to supporting Sarah’s blonde,” Moe says.
Want the Look?
For anyone newly inspired by Carolyn’s polished warmth, Hill suggests bringing a reference photo to the salon and asking specifically about her Foiled Cashmere technique.

The key, Moe says, is deciding which Carolyn you’re drawn to. The earlier, more dimensional blond is “a little bit less committed,” while the later version requires more upkeep and frequent trips to the salon to deliver that unmistakable root-to-tip finish.

Maintenance, Hill emphasizes, is what keeps the blond from tipping into brassiness or looking overworked. On set, she relied on Schwarzkopf’s BLONDME lightener and IGORA Vibrance for toning, along with the brand’s BLONDME Care range to maintain shine and strength between appointments. At home, products like the BLONDME Sealing Balm and the Bondfinity Deep Repair Oil helped reinforce the hair’s bonds during filming, when styling was constant and unforgiving.

And perhaps the most important takeaway: Carolyn’s blond was never accidental. The irony of “iconic without effort” is that the effort is simply invisible, layered in foil by foil, until all you see is confidence.
And that part, no bottle can replicate.

title: Kathie Lee Gifford Is Making a Documentary About Her Life: 'Chance To Set the Record Straight'
summary: Kathie Lee Gifford is ready to tell the story of her remarkable life in a documentary.
story: Kathie Lee Gifford is ready to tell the story of her remarkable life in a documentary.

TODAY.com confirms that Emmy Award-winning director Nick Nanton and executive producer and reality mogul Kris Jenner are creating a documentary about the former TODAY co-host. The film is still being shot and does not have a distributor yet.
Kathie Lee, 72, spoke about the documentary with her friend and former co-host Hoda Kotb on TODAY March 9. She said her son Cody, 35, nudged her to do the film.
"Oh gosh, I had to look at a final cut the other day pretty much, and I go, ‘How did I do it? And what happened to me? I used to look kind of good! What the heck,'" Kathie Lee joked.
Cody inspired the mother of two and grandmother of five to participate in the film as a legacy project for her family.

"But when you look at your life in total ... Cody said, ‘Mom, I know you don’t want to do that. But I want you to, and Cassidy wants you to, and do it for your grandchildren because you’re not going to always be there,'" Kathie Lee said. "And it’s your chance to set the record straight about an awful lot of things."
"Not that I care as much as I once did about that sort of thing," she continued. "You get to the point where you don’t care. Small amount of people that you actually still truly trust."

Kathie Lee said the documentary is “not coming for a while.” Her diverse career has included iconic stints as a morning talk show host on "Live! With Regis and Kathie Lee" from 1985 to 2000 and on TODAY from 2008 through 2019. She has released multiple albums as a singer, been the face of Carnival Cruise Lines commercials, starred on stage in musical theater and appeared onscreen in a Hallmark Christmas movie and other shows.
She was married to NFL and broadcasting legend Frank Gifford from 1986 until his death at 84 in 2015.
Kathie Lee has inspired generations of talk show hosts and reality stars, including Hoda and Kris Jenner. Hoda told her on TODAY that she credits much of her career to Kathie Lee.

"You owe me everything," Kathie Lee joked. "Kris Jenner says the same thing in the documentary. She says ‘I owe my whole career to Kathie.' Don’t say that, because I’ll get blamed, and I’ll be thanked."
Her most recent projects have been books inspired by Biblical stories. Her latest novel, “Nero & Paul,” was released on March 10.
“There’s a lot going on, but these books keep me active,” Kathie Lee said. “They keep my mind incredibly active.”
Kathie Lee also cited Cody as an influence on her writing career.

"This is all Cody's fault. He said, 'Mom, you know how you're so fascinated with these epic stories in the Bible? Mom, let's make thrillers out of them,'" she said. "I try to combine my love of history, my love of the word of God, with putting on a good show."

title: ‘Love Is Blind’ Season 10 Couples: Who’s Still Together?
summary: Where do all relationships and friendships from "Love Is Blind" Season 10 stand today? Read on to find out what each couple revealed at the reunion.
story: “Love Is Blind” Season 10 ended happily, with two couples saying “I do” and dancing the night away at their wedding receptions. But the drama was far from over.

The two married couples and the five other pairs that got engaged during the Ohio-based season of the Netflix dating show reunited to update viewers about their relationships nearly a year after filming ended.

As the reunion began, hosts Nick and Vanessa Lachey welcomed the Season 10 stars to the stage, but not everyone remained throughout the hour-long episode. As contestants were called out about their behavior on and off the show, tears were shed and one stormed off the set.

Plus, multiple contestants had to defend their actions in front of the live audience, which was filled with some “Love Is Blind” alums. So where do all relationships and friendships stand today? Read on to find out what each couple shared about their current relationship statuses.
Jordan and Amber
Relationship status: Not together
Jordan revealed that their marriage lasted four months and they are no longer together.
Jordan, when speaking about the breakup, pointed to Amber's trust issues and "damage done" from past relationships. "I was constantly trying to repair a trust that I didn't break," he said.
"There were some ups, for sure, but for me the negatives outweighed the positives," he said.
Amber, meanwhile, said she didn't think Jordan was ready for the relationship. He wanted to be at home in Columbus more than at home with Amber, he said.
Amber also accused Jordan of not saying goodbye to Emma after their breakup conversation. "Do you know how hurt she was about that?" she said.
"I don't know how to handle that," Jordan said.
Amber ended up storming off from the set after becoming upset about Emma's reaction to Jordan leaving.
She did return and got a pep talk from the other women. Amber concluded that she thinks Jordan is a "good guy," but threw in a dig at Chris.
Vic and Christine
Relationship status: Still together

Vanessa Lachey called Vic and Christine's relationship "one of the most pure" in the entire show. The two are still going strong after falling in love in the pods. Their major update was that they are now living together in Columbus. They seem as happy as ever.
Chris and Jess
Relationship status: Not together
Chris spent the reunion owning up to his behavior on the show.
He broke up with Jess after saying he was used to women who do Crossfit. He also alienated the men of the group by going after Bri in front of her partner, Connor.
At Nick Lachey's prompting, he apologized to Connor and Bri, then moved on to the Jess of it all.
Jess said she thought Chris had "deep insecurities." He responded by saying he prioritized physical chemistry over emotional chemistry but should have gone about it a different way.
Jess thanked him for his apology and for "showing me who you were quickly."
Jess revealed she is in a relationship with fellow cast member, and doctor, Haramol Gill.
Connor and Bri
Relationship status: Still together
Connor and Bri chose not to get married in the finale. Afterward, Connor said they embarked on a "new chapter," living like a normal couple. He moved into a home next door to hers.
Bri said she thinks her relationship with Connor is going all the way to the altar.
"Connor loved me for everything I was in that experiment ... it was not the best version of myself," she said. "I can't marry you as this person right now because I was spiraling."
She said they're living their "best days" as a couple. "He's the best. I love everything about him and he loves everything about me. That's the goal," he said.
Devonta and Brittany
Relationship status: Not together
Brittany and Devonta broke up ahead of the weddings. Devonta owned up to the fact that he could have been more upfront with his doubts.
They also sparred over a trip that Devonta took to Austin with other cast members, after telling Brittany it was for a "work trip." Brittany's father also confronted him.
Devonta dropped the major news that he is engaged and has a child on the way. Brittany is single.
Mike and Emma
Relationship status: Not together
At the reunion, Mike and Emma went over the lead-up to their decision day, where Mike said no and Emma said yes.
Mike said that they had agreed to say no and continue to explore their relationship. He was caught off guard when she said yes.
"In the moment, I could not wholeheartedly say yes," he said.

Emma, meanwhile, said she felt trust and love for Mike, and felt like she committed to having kids — their one big disagreement — with her yes. "I thought saying yes in front of your family and my family was a declaration of kids," she said.
Emma said she's single. Mike, meanwhile, brought his girlfriend of seven months to the filming.
Alex and Ashley
Relationship status: Not together
The two continued to rehash the many disagreements about Alex's life story at the reunion.

title: When Is the 'Love Is Blind' Season 10 Reunion?
summary: What time is the Love Is Blind season 10 reunion? How do you watch the Love Is Blind reunion? Find out details for the reunion.
story: Only two couples said “I do” at the end of “Love Is Blind” Season 10. Now, viewers will get an update on where those marriages stand when the reunion special premieres.
Ever since the Season 10 finale hit Netflix on March 4, viewers have been curious to know what happened to this season’s couples after the cameras stopped rolling.
During the upcoming reunion special, Season 10 cast members will dish on their relationship status and offer new insight into their experience filming “Love Is Blind.”
Nick and Vanessa Lachey are set to return as hosts of the reunion special and will ask cast members some challenging questions.
Wondering how to tune in to the reunion? Here’s everything you need to know.
When Is the ‘Love Is Blind’ Season 10 Reunion?
The Season 10 “Love Is Blind” reunion special will hit Netflix on Wednesday, March 11 at 6 p.m. PT / 9 p.m. ET.
What Will Happen During the Season 10 Reunion?
A trailer for the reunion promises lots of drama.
Chris Fusco, who caught criticism for comments about his fiancée Jessica Barrett’s body, addresses his behavior in the clip, saying, “I’m not proud of a lot of those moments.”
Jessica then speaks, saying, “The reality was very, very different than what was being shown.”

Brittany Wicker, who was engaged to Devonta Anderson before the couple opted out of walking down the aisle, alludes to some drama in one clip as she says, “Did you not know that I had my engagement ring on?”
Amber Morrison, who married Jordan Faeth in the season finale, is seen storming off the stage as she says, “I told you, don’t f------ do that.”
One other marriage came out of Season 10: Victor St. John and Christine Hamilton. The couple will appear in the reunion alongside the other Season 10 cast members.

title: Donna Kelce Explains What She's Actually Doing to Her Home
summary: Donna Kelce responded to her home renovations and explained what she is actually doing. She is adding windows.
story: Mama Kelce is setting the record straight after reports about her rumored home renovations caused a stir this week.
In a new episode of the “New Heights” podcast, Jason and Travis Kelce called their mom Donna Kelce to ask her to weigh in on the hot topic.
Jason began by asking his mom about “this tweet that has taken the world by storm.”
“What the heck? Even TMZ sent me a text and said, ‘Can you be on? This is really funny.’ I’m like, ‘It’s so stupid,’” Donna Kelce replied.

In case you missed it, Donna Kelce went viral earlier this week following a report from TMZ that she was remodeling her “modest” Florida home. In response, Jason Kelce joked about the rumors by sharing the report and tweeting, “BREAKING!! Sources confirm Donna Kelce’s eldest son watched 2 hours of Bluey yesterday.”
When the “New Heights” Instagram page reposted his funny message, Donna Kelce followed suit and shared it on her Instagram page, but didn’t offer any comment.

Hilarity ensued as social media users feigned extreme interest in the rumored renovations. The X account for the Empire State Building also got in on the fun, congratulating Donna Kelce on her remodel.
While chatting with her sons, Donna Kelce said she thinks she knows where the rumors first started.
“They probably pulled a permit because they’re coming on Monday to put my windows and doors in,” she said.
While Jason and Donna Kelce were discussing the gossip du jour, Travis Kelce was making some epic silly faces with his niece, who appeared in the background right behind her father.
Jason was curious to know if this was a “random” renovation or if his mother was filming something like an “HGTV show.” She confirmed that it was just a minor update.
“All I’m doing is my people are coming to put in my windows on Monday,” she said.
“Wait, you’re doing 16 windows? The article says nine,” Jason Kelce joked.
“It’s because you know my whole apartment is all windows,” Donna Kelce explained.
Jason Kelce took the opportunity to tease his mother a bit.
“Well, I just wanted to say, mom, they said ‘modest home’ and it feels like you’re turning this place into a bit of a gilded palace. I think you’ve changed a little bit,” he joked.
Donna Kelce laughed and said the minor improvements are pretty practical.
“I’m doing something so that I can save money on heating and air conditioning and to keep my windows from sweating. That’s as simple as it is,” she said.
Jason Kelce said the whole situation was “hilarious” and his mother agreed.
“Oh, it’s so funny,” she said.
Jason Kelce’s wife, Kylie Kelce, also weighed in on the rumors this week in an episode of her podcast, “Not Gonna Lie with Kylie Kelce,” calling the news “shocking” and “groundbreaking.”
The mother of four said that her mother-in-law had given her a heads up that she was installing new windows at home.

“And that was a little earth-shattering for me because when I heard that she had scheduled this really just crazy remodel of getting her windows replaced, I thought, ‘Well done, Donna. Way to do something for yourself,’” she said in a clip from the podcast.
Kylie Kelce kept up the act, saying it was “really, really important that this hit the internet when it did.”
“I think that the gravity of this article was important to address,” she teased.

title: Rudy Pankow Recalls Tan that Went Wrong for ‘Reminders of Him’ Nude Scene
summary: Rudy Pankow discussed how his attempt to get tan for his movie "Reminders of Him" went off the rails.
story: It wasn’t just embarrassment that had Rudy Pankow feeling red when it came to shooting one scene from his new movie.
Pankow, who stars in the big screen adaptation of Colleen Hoover's book “Reminders of Him,” revealed on TODAY March 11 that he got a bad case of sunburn while prepping for the film.
To get ready for a scene set by a lake that involved nudity, he said he decided to try and get some color before filming.
“I told my girlfriend, ‘I’m going to go out and lay in the backyard.’ And she was like, ‘OK, just don’t be out there too long,'" he recalled.
Things didn’t go according to plan, though, because he spent two and a half hours face down outside without sunscreen.
“I wanted to commit,” he joked.
That mistake turned his backside a color more reserved for tomatoes.
“She got back, and she was like, ‘I thought I saw two red floaties in the pool or something.’ And I ran inside. I looked like a baboon,” he said.
Things didn’t get much better, either, when it came time for the “Outer Banks” star to shoot his big revealing scene.
“It just got more red. And then I started to peel,” he said.
Eventually, his girlfriend stepped in.

“My girlfriend was like, ‘You need to tell hair and makeup now.’ So I was like, ‘OK.’ I walk into hair and makeup like a dog with its tail between his legs,” he said. “I was like, ‘Guys, I did something, and with your consent, can I show you my butt?’

"And immediately, immediately, everyone knew ... and they were so professional. And they’re like, ‘How bad Rudy?’ And I’m like, ‘I’ll just show you.’ And they’re like, ‘That’s bad. That’s bad,'"  he continued.
But the hair and makeup team managed to rectify the problem.
“They took the tanner and sprayed me down like a car, and then my cheeks never looked better onscreen,” Pankow said.
You can see Pankow and his non-red posterior when “Reminders of Him,” also starring Lauren Graham, Lainey Wilson, Tyriq Withers and Maika Monroe, opens in theaters on March 13.

title: Use the stairs, work from home: Asia is already making big changes as oil prices spike
summary: The fallout of the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran and the subsequent effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has reached Asia with authorities from New Delhi to Manila implementing emergency measures to shield consumers from the mounting shortages and surging global oil prices.
story: HONG KONG — The fallout from the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is being felt sharply in Asia, with authorities from New Delhi to Manila implementing emergency measures to shield consumers from mounting shortages and surging oil prices.

On Friday, people in Nepal lined up at gas-filling stations, carrying their empty, red cooking-gas cylinders as the country’s main oil company said it would only fill them halfway with LPG, or liquefied petroleum gas, as it tries to make stocks last longer.

Neighboring India, which is the world’s second-largest importer of LPG after China, is grappling with panic-buying among its own citizens amid wild swings in the price of international Brent crude oil, which as of early Friday was above $100 a barrel.

The unease highlights just how much a region dependent on oil from Gulf nations is affected by the Iran war, which the International Energy Agency says has created the “largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.”

Unlike the U.S. or Europe, which have more diverse sources of oil, Asia relies heavily on imports that pass through the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route along southern Iran that carries about a fifth of the world’s oil.

“The ability to refine different oils from different places is complicated and not easily shifted in Asia,” Robert Savage, the head of markets strategy and insight at Bank of New York Mellon, told NBC News on Thursday.
Among the countries most affected are Singapore, Thailand, South Korea, Pakistan and Japan, according to a research note Thursday from Eurasia Group, a New York-based geopolitical risk analysis firm.
The escalating conflict in the Middle East has set off an energy frenzy across the continent, forcing governments to ration fuel and scramble for alternative supplies.

In India, which has invoked emergency powers directing refineries to maximize LPG production, oil companies say they are focused on ensuring the stability of domestic supplies, including to essential services such as hospitals.

Though authorities have said the measures are precautionary and that India has plenty of oil, panic-buying threatens to strain domestic resources. “We request everyone not to believe such rumors or crowd fuel stations unnecessarily,” Mumbai-based Bharat Petroleum said on X Saturday.
Even as India struggles to reassure its own population, it is getting additional pressure from its South Asian neighbors.
Bangladesh, Sri Lanka and the Maldives have all requested supplies from New Delhi, Indian foreign ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said Thursday.

Muslim-majority Bangladesh also closed universities and brought forward the Eid al-Fitr holidays to save electricity and fuel. Worried consumers have flocked to gas stations to fill up their vehicles.
In Southeast Asia, the Philippines has initiated a four-day workweek for government employees, while Vietnam has urged its citizens to work from home and limit vehicle usage.

title: Ukraine's anti-drone tech is in high demand as Iran attacks its neighbors
summary: KYIV, Ukraine — As the conflict in the Middle East escalates, Ukraine could prove to be an invaluable trove of battle-tested expertise from its own bitter and costly fight against Russia
story: KYIV, Ukraine — As the conflict in the Middle East escalates, Ukraine could prove to be an invaluable trove of battle-tested expertise from its own bitter and costly fight against Russia.

After months of pressure and hardened rhetoric from Washington aimed at ending the war in Ukraine, Kyiv is now also fielding requests for help as Iran’s Gulf neighbors grapple with the modern reality of drone warfare.

Hotels, airports and residential buildings have been hit in cities across the Gulf, wreaking havoc as Iran targets the U.S. military bases hosted by its neighbors. It’s a picture all too familiar in Ukraine, whose skies are swarmed by hundreds of Russian drones on a nightly basis, many of them of the Shahed type designed in Iran.

Kyiv’s forces deflect the majority of them every night, not with expensive air defense missiles — as many countries in the Middle East have done — but with much cheaper and more effective interceptor drones, technology honed and perfected by four years of intense drone warfare.

The raging war has made Ukraine a unique “ecosystem” that allows for real-time testing of innovative drone technology on the battlefield, said Marko Kushnir, a spokesperson for General Cherry, one of Ukraine’s top drone manufacturers.

“The feedback loop between the front and the manufacturer is very short,” Kushnir said. “We can get feedback in the morning, and in the evening have a solution that will be tackling new tasks on the battlefield.”

Created in 2023 by a group of veterans and volunteers, the Kyiv-based company produces close to 100,000 drones a month, Kushnir said. One of its flagship interceptor drones specifically stops Shaheds and is actively used by Ukraine’s armed forces, he added. The company was invited to take part in the Pentagon’s $1 billion Drone Dominance initiative before the Iran war broke out.
It’s a level of expertise that Ukraine has paid heavily for, Kushnir said — “with lives, territory and a very long war with a bigger, better-resourced enemy.”
There are two countries in the world that understand from experience how to fight a daily, grinding technological war with drones, he said. “That’s us and Russia,” Kushnir added.
Ukraine’s allies recognize that now, he said.

President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said earlier this week that he received nearly a dozen requests from the U.S. and countries in the Middle East and Europe for “Ukraine’s experience in protecting lives, relevant interceptors, electronic warfare systems, and training.”

Ukraine’s experience in countering attack drones is “irreplaceable,” he said in a series of posts on X, and “the most advanced in the world.” Kyiv has dispatched teams to the Middle East, Zelenskyy said on Wednesday, raising the prospect of a weapons exchange, with Ukraine desperate for air defense missiles to counter the sophisticated weapons that Russia is using against Ukrainian cities and critical infrastructure on a near daily basis.

“This is not about being involved in operations. ​We are not at ​war with ⁠Iran,” Zelenskyy told Reuters in comments released Sunday. “This is about protection and a thorough, complete assessment on our part of how to counter the ​Shaheds.”
He ⁠said that what Ukraine will get in return for the assistance still needed to be discussed, adding: “Honestly, for us today, both the technology and the funding ⁠are important.”

title: U.S. oil soars past $100 a barrel, as Iran war shows no signs of ending soon
summary: U.S. crude oil passed $100 per barrel Sunday, continuing its surge despite efforts by the Trump administration and allied countries to slow rising prices.
story: U.S. crude oil hit $100 per barrel Sunday, continuing its surge as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran shows no signs of ending soon and despite attempts by the Trump administration and allied countries to slow rising prices.
As oil futures trading resumed trading at 6 p.m. ET, U.S. crude oil jumped more than 2%, to around $102 per barrel. The international oil benchmark, Brent crude, also jumped, to $106 per barrel.
On Wednesday, the 32 countries that make up the International Energy Agency unanimously agreed to release a collective 400 million barrels of oil in the largest emergency release ever.

Lawmakers, investors and consumers alike had all hoped the move would cut prices and reassure the markets. Oil prices momentarily fell below $80 a barrel after the announcement, before they resumed their steady march higher.
Since the war started, U.S. oil prices have risen nearly 50%. So far this year, the cost of U.S. crude has risen nearly 75%.

Retail gas prices have soared, too. On Sunday, the national average price per gallon of unleaded gas was around $3.70, up about 70 cents since the U.S. and Israel launched wide-scale strikes on targets in Iran.
Retaliatory Iranian attacks across the region on ships, infrastructure and ports through which oil tankers transit have only deepened fears that the war will become a protracted regional conflict.
In the meantime, the critical Strait of Hormuz — through which more than 20% of the world's oil supply must pass to reach global markets — will remain closed for the foreseeable future.
On Friday, the U.S. intensified its military pressure on Iran, striking Kharg Island, a crucial outpost in the Persian Gulf from which Iran exports around 90% of its oil.
Trump said the strikes did not affect the island's oil infrastructure. He told NBC News in a phone interview Saturday, "We may hit [Kharg Island] a few more times, just for fun.”
He also said he was not yet ready to negotiate a cease-fire with Iran.
Trump said in the interview that he was asking “numerous countries that are affected by the thuggery of Iran” to help secure the strait.
The White House has been dangling the prospect of naval escorts to protect oil tankers for more than a week, without providing any details yet.
“We have never had the most important waterway for energy effectively closed,” RBC Capital Markets’ chief commodities strategist Helima Croft told NBC News last week.
"The only way this crisis abates is if there is some way that we can reopen the Strait of Hormuz and give confidence to shipping companies that their tankers will not be attacked," Croft said.
On NBC’s “Meet the Press” earlier Sunday, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said it could still be a number of weeks before military escorts are possible.

Wright was asked whether the Strait of Hormuz is safe for ships right now. "No, no, it is not,” he said. Making it safe for ships to pass is “one of the objectives at the end of this conflict," he said.
Until the war with Iran ends, Wright said, "we’re going to see some elevated pricing.”
Some analysts say the $100 mark, which West Texas Intermediate crude oil crossed Sunday evening, could be oil's first stop on the road to even higher prices.

"The psychological level of $100 oil may just be a short-term price target on its way to higher levels as the conflict drags on, oil production is throttled back as oil storage fills up because tankers are unable to load," longtime industry expert Andy Lipow said last week.

title: Pentagon identifies 6 U.S. crew members killed in refueling plane crash
summary: The six U.S. crew members who died when their military refueling plane crashed in Iraq while taking part in Iran war operations this week were publicly identified Saturday
story: The six U.S. crew members who died when their military refueling plane crashed in Iraq while taking part in Iran war operations this week were publicly identified Saturday.

The Defense Department identified the U.S. Air Force airmen as Maj. John A. Klinner, 33, of Auburn, Alabama; Capt. Ariana G. Savino, 31, of Covington, Washington; Tech. Sgt. Ashley B.
